agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: North Road BIA renewal blocked by property owner petitions
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA is a zone where local businesses pay an extra levy (a special tax) to fund improvements like marketing, street cleaning, or events. However, enough property owners in the area have submitted formal petitions against the renewal. Under the rules, if a sufficient number of owners object, the renewal cannot go ahead unless council votes to override those objections. Council will now consider the petition results and decide whether to proceed with the renewal bylaw or let it fail.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the BIA renewal is blocked, your levy may not continue.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal: 66 petitions oppose, council advised not to adopt
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a Certificate of Sufficiency showing that 66 Notices of Opposition were received from property owners representing 65 of 120 folio numbers within the proposed North Road BIA renewal boundary. The petition threshold requires at least 50% of parcels and 50% of assessed value to block adoption; the report advises council not to proceed with the renewal bylaw. The proposed 5-year term was April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031, with levies based on Class 5 (Light Industrial) and Class 6 (Business) property assessments.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — if the bylaw is not adopted, the BIA renewal will not proceed and levy payments will not change.

key_dates.label: Council vote on North Road BIA renewal bylaw

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a staff report certifying that petitions against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw (Bylaw #14797) are sufficient. The petition period ran February 25 to March 30, 2026. Of 121 property owners notified, 66 submitted Notices of Opposition covering 65 folio numbers — meeting the 50% parcel and 50% assessed value thresholds required to block adoption. Staff recommend Council not proceed with the renewal bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw. These changes would launch a new E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the city. If approved, the new rules would allow shared e-bikes and e-scooters to operate on city streets and in parks, with specific parking and safety rules. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if you operate a business that could be affected by e-bike or e-scooter parking or traffic changes.
agenda_briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE
PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER
PILOT PROGRAM

Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the
Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program
and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendments proposed
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to allow an e-bike share pilot program and address the provincial electric kick scooter pilot program. Key rules include: shared e-bikes and e-scooters must park only in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; e-scooters are banned on major road networks and arterial roads except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; no riding on sidewalks unless permitted by a traffic control device; and no riding while wearing headphones over both ears. Violations of moving vehicle rules are enforced by RCMP under the Motor Vehicle Act.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — you may want to comment if your business is affected by e-bike or e-scooter parking or traffic rules.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the submission of Burnaby's first 6-month Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This report is required under the provincial Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation. The report shows that 910 net new housing units were completed in the first six months of the target period. If approved, this report will be forwarded to the province as part of the city's obligation to show progress on meeting provincially set housing targets.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on joining Building Benchmark BC Year 7 program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC program. This is a voluntary provincial program that tracks energy and water use in large buildings. If approved, the city would sign up to share building performance data with the province. The goal is to help building owners save money on utilities and reduce greenhouse gas emissions. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, your building may need to start tracking energy and water use.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a bylaw creating a Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road. minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for 2026 election
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved the appointment of Dennis Back as the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. This means Mr. Back will be responsible for overseeing the planning and running of the next municipal election, ensuring it is conducted fairly and according to the rules. The decision was made at a council meeting and is now final.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report recommending the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 165
THAT Dennis Back be appointed Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved North Road BIA renewal blocked by sufficient petitions
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received a report confirming that a sufficient number of property owners in the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) filed petitions opposing the renewal of the BIA for 2026. Because the petitions met the legal threshold, the BIA renewal cannot proceed. Council voted unanimously to accept the Certificate of Sufficiency and to send a copy of the report to the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association. This means the BIA will not be renewed for 2026, and property owners in the area will not be charged the associated levy.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a property owner in the North Road BIA, no action is needed — the renewal is blocked. Contact the City of Burnaby if you have questions about your property's BIA status.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.2 CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 166
THAT the report titled "Certificate of Sufficiency: North Road BIA Renewal" dated April 14, 2026 be received for information;
THAT the Certificate of Sufficiency dated April 14, 2026, certified by the Corporate Officer and included as Attachment 1 to this report, be received; and
THAT a copy of this report be sent to the President of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved North Road BIA renewal for another five years
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for a five-year term starting in 2026. A BIA is a defined area where local businesses pay a special levy (a fee added to their property taxes) to fund marketing, beautification, and other improvements that benefit the whole business district. Council received a Certificate of Sufficiency, which confirms that enough property owners signed petitions in support of the renewal to move forward. The decision is final.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own a business or property in the North Road BIA, expect your annual BIA levy to continue for the next five years — budget accordingly.

key_dates.label: North Road BIA renewal term begins

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 2026 corporate maintenance for Burnaby Housing Authority
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the 2026 corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA), the city's housing corporation. Council authorized the annual general meeting to be handled through a unanimous shareholder resolution, set December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, appointed KPMG as the auditor, received the BHA's 2025 financial statements, and appointed new directors. This decision was carried unanimously.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 2026 corporate maintenance matters for Burnaby Housing Authority
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the annual shareholder's corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026. The Shareholder Representative was directed to execute the necessary resolutions.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bylaw amendments for e-bike share and e-scooter pilot programs
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to authorize the City Solicitor to bring forward amendments to three bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program. The changes will affect the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw, and the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This means new rules for where e-bikes and e-scooters can be ridden, parked, and how violations will be enforced are now being finalized.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaws once they are passed to understand where e-bikes and e-scooters can be used and parked on Burnaby streets and in parks.

THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the following bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program:
• Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961;
• Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979; and
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,

minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved amendments to the Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to allow the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program to operate in the city. The changes update the rules for where e-bikes and e-scooters can be used, parked, and enforced, including on streets and in parks. The decision is final and the bylaw amendments will now be drafted and brought into effect.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved hiring an independent external reviewer to examine the city's financial planning, operating practices, and how it sets priorities for capital investments. The goal is to protect the city's assets, avoid major tax hikes, and keep service costs from rising for businesses and residents. The reviewer will work with the Financial Management Committee and report directly to Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES

The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report seeking Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 181
THAT engaging a qualified external reviewer to assess the City’s financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting, as discussed at the Financial Management Committee meeting held on March 17, 2026, be approved; and
THAT the reviewer liaise with the Financial Management Committee and deliver findings and recommendations directly to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council abandons Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw after petitions
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to abandon the proposed Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026. The bylaw was dropped because the city received enough petitions from property owners in the area to stop it. A Business Improvement Area (BIA) is a special zone where local businesses pay extra fees to fund improvements and marketing for the area. Since the bylaw was abandoned, the North Road BIA will not be created.
minutes_briefs.source_body:

Resolution No. 2026 - 185
THAT due to sufficient petitions received by the City against Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, Bylaw No. 14797 be now abandoned.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby council refers PST expansion opposition to staff for review
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received a letter from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business (CFIB) urging the City to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax (PST) expansion on professional services. Council voted unanimously to refer the letter to staff for further review and to report back. This means the City will study the potential impact of the PST expansion on local businesses before deciding on any formal position.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required. Monitor for the staff report and any future council decision on this matter.
minutes_briefs.source_body:

Arising from discussion, the following motion was brought forward:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2025 - 186
